title: Construction-specific agreement listing baseline
notes: Sixty-second-round rival baseline generated after external critique noted that AGR had beaten a simple surface-head baseline but not a construction-specific listing account. This graph deliberately collapses controller identification, notional agreement basis, and override machinery into stored local agreement preferences. It is a live rival to AGR for known item profiles, not a scoped module and not a general grammaticality account.

edges.rationale: Observed local agreement distributions provide warrant for a stored construction-specific preference.
A learned local preference raises production probability for the preferred agreement value in that lexical or constructional frame.
A stable local preference can provide contextual warrant for licensing in an already-attested constructional niche, without explaining why the preference generalizes.
Reported acceptability can indicate licensing but is biased by task framing, familiarity, and ideology.
Community licensing provides warrant for agreement-related grammaticality attribution without being identical to the attribution.
Standard-language ideology can raise explicit condemnation of familiar agreement variants independently of production probability.
Condemnation can influence attribution while remaining separable from distributional production preferences.
